body.title: After-sprint skill: refresh clan.code_index for committed files
body.result: step 10.5 added to sprint-ticket skill: git diff HEAD~1 HEAD | code_indexer --files; synced to ~/.claude/skills
body.intention: I intend that clan.code_index stays current after every sprint by refreshing only the files the commit touched, keeping orientation classifier accuracy high without a full nightly re-index on every change.
body.description:
After a sprint commits and pushes, the files changed in that commit should be re-indexed immediately so the orientation classifier sees the new symbols on the next ticket. The nightly Nanny sweep covers everything but fires too late for same-session accuracy.

Add a step to the after-sprint skill (or an after-commit hook) that: (1) reads the list of files in the most recent commit (`git diff --name-only HEAD~1 HEAD`), (2) calls the code_indexer for each file, (3) writes updated rows to clan.code_index. Shell/bash files trigger the Haiku intent pass (from T-symbols-table-multilang).

This is a light incremental update — typically 3–10 files per sprint. Non-fatal if the indexer fails; log and continue.

**Affected files:** skills/sprint-ticket (after-sprint steps), lab/claudecode/code_indexer.py, clan.code_index rows
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; log every DB write; non-fatal (never blocks commit/push)
**Scope boundary:** In scope: post-commit incremental index update for files in diff. Out of scope: full re-index (Nanny's job), embedding refresh.
**Completion criteria:** After a sprint commit, `SELECT updated_at FROM clan.code_index WHERE file_path = '<changed_file>'` shows a timestamp within 60s of the commit; skill step appears in sprint-ticket skill after the push step; all tests pass.
